messages.content:
Which of the following are common uses of SharePoint?


Collaborative Software

Enterprise Document Management

Video/Audio Calls

Email management
Collaborative Software and Enterprise Document Management are common uses of SharePoint. Video/Audio Calls and Email management are not typically considered core functionalities of SharePoint.

Enabling file check - out prevents multiple people from making changes at the same time to avoid editing conflicts.


True

False
True. Enabling file check-out in SharePoint prevents multiple people from making changes to the same document or file at the same time, which helps to avoid editing conflicts.

Select the scenarios in which versions are created by SharePoint.


Upload/creation of a file.

Change in properties of a file.

Deleting a file.

Opening and closing a file.

Versions are created by SharePoint in the following scenarios:

- Upload/creation of a file.
- Change in properties of a file.
- Opening and closing a file.

Deleting a file does not create a new version in SharePoint, but it can potentially affect the version history if the file is restored from the recycle bin or a backup.

A user should add a _________ page if they want to present content in the "What You See Is What You Get" format.


site

wiki

web part

none of the above
A user should add a Wiki page if they want to present content in the "What You See Is What You Get" (WYSIWYG) format.

A logical grouping of one or more SharePoint servers is known as a farm.


True

False
True. In SharePoint, a logical grouping of one or more servers that work together to provide a set of SharePoint services and capabilities is known as a farm.

By default, permissions in SharePoint Online are inherited.


True

False

True. By default, permissions in SharePoint Online are inherited, meaning that any sub-site, folder, or item within a site collection inherits the permissions of its parent site. However, permissions can also be customized at any level within a site collection if required.

A user needs to be given access to view, add, update and delete list items and documents. What level of access should be assigned?

Design

Contribute

Edit

Read

The user should be assigned the Contribute level of access in order to view, add, update, and delete list items and documents in SharePoint. This permission level allows users to contribute to existing content, but not make any structural changes to the site or manage permissions.

If the user asks you a question that you are not aware of, which of the following statements would you say to enhance customer service experience?


I would have helped you, but I don’t know.

That’s a good question. Let me check that for you.

Give me a moment please.

I apologize. Since I am new here, I am not sure of this.

That's a good question. Let me check that for you. 

This response shows that you are willing to help and take the time to find a solution for the user, which enhances the customer service experience.

If the user asks you a question that you are not aware of, which of the following statements would you say to enhance customer service experience?


I would have helped you, but I don’t know.

That’s a good question. Let me check that for you.

Give me a moment please.

I apologize. Since I am new here, I am not sure of this.

That's a good question. Let me check that for you.

This response shows that you are willing to help and take the time to find a solution for the user, which enhances the customer service experience.

Everybody ___ to wear an ID card. (Select the option basis Subject Verb Agreement).


need

should have 

needs

all of the above

The correct option basis Subject Verb Agreement is: needs.

The sentence subject "everybody" is a singular pronoun, so it requires a singular verb form. In this case, the correct verb form is "needs". "Need" is incorrect because it is a plural verb form, and "should have" is also grammatically incorrect in this sentence because it is not a verb form.

The user ___ to contact the local helpdesk.


needs

need

must

have

The correct option based on the sentence is: needs.

The subject "the user" is a singular noun, so it requires a singular verb form. In this case, the correct verb form is "needs". "Need" is incorrect because it is a plural verb form, "must" is incorrect because it implies an obligation or requirement, which may not be the case in this sentence, and "have" is also incorrect because it is not a verb form that agrees with the singular subject.

I have saved the excel file ___ the folder.


in

on

inside

into

The correct option based on the sentence is: in.

The preposition "in" indicates that the excel file is located within the boundaries or confines of the folder. The other prepositions could also be used in certain contexts, but "in" is the most common and appropriate choice in this sentence.

Which among the following would be an ideal opening statement to your email?


Please be informed that there is an issue with my Microsoft Teams application.

Kindly note that my Microsoft Teams application is not working.

I am writing this email to inform you that my Microsoft Teams is not working!

I am writing this email to inform you that my Microsoft Teams is not working.

The ideal opening statement to your email among the given options is: 

"I am writing this email to inform you that my Microsoft Teams is not working!"

This opening statement is polite, professional, and clearly communicates the purpose of the email. Options 1 and 2 are also acceptable but using "Please be informed" or "Kindly note" might sound a bit too formal in some contexts. Option 3 is not appropriate as it starts with the contraction "I'm" instead of "I am," which is more formal.

If the user requests for the issue to be resolved immediately within ten minutes for a low priority ticket, how would you communicate this to the user assuming that the turn-around-time for such tickets is 24 hours?


I wish I could do that for you; however, the turn-around-time for this ticket is 24 hours. What I could do for you is I will leave a note on the ticket that this is important and someone will contact you shortly.

I am afraid that I cannot that for you; however, the turn-around-time for this ticket is 24 hours. What I could do for you is I will leave a note on the ticket that this is important and someone will contact you shortly.

I'm really sorry, I'm helpless here as the turn around time is 24 hours and cannot be resolved in 10 minutes. Please have patience, and we will resolve it. Don't worry.

Don't worry, we will resolve it as per our timelines.

The ideal response to the user under the given scenario could be:

"I understand that your request is urgent, however, the turn-around-time for low priority tickets is 24 hours. We will definitely resolve your issue within that time frame. However, I will leave a note on the ticket to highlight the urgency of your request so that our team can prioritize it accordingly and get back to you as soon as possible."

This response acknowledges the user's urgency while also managing their expectations in a professional and polite manner. It also assures the user that their issue will be resolved within the standard timeline for low-priority tickets, but the request will be marked as urgent to ensure that it gets attention as soon as possible.

If you need a leave and need to write to your supervisor, what would the subject line be?


Attention needed: Leave Request

Do not delete: Leave request

Leave Request: <Date>

My leave plan

The ideal subject line for an email requesting leave would be:

"Leave Request: <Date>"

This subject line clearly indicates the purpose of the email, which is to request leave, and includes the date(s) for which leave is being requested. This makes it easier for the supervisor to identify and prioritize the email among other messages. The other options don't convey the purpose or urgency of the email as effectively as this one.

Choose the ideal paraphrasing statement if the user says the following: This morning when I switched on the computer, I was just able to see a blue screen. I thought the wires were not connected properly, so I kind of checked them and they seemed to be fine. I requested a friend of mine to re-connect the wires once again, but I still got to see the blue screen. My colleague told me to contact you guys. I am not quite sure if you can help me. There are some strange messages on my screen as well.


I will certainly help you with this and I appreciate your effort in basic troubleshooting. As I understand, when you switch on the computer, you are able to see a blue screen with an error message, am I right?

I apologize for the inconvenience and I appreciate your effort in basic troubleshooting. As I understand, when you turn on the computer, you are getting a blue screen with an error message, am I right?

I apologize for the inconvenience that has been caused and I appreciate your effort in basic troubleshooting. Correct me if I am wrong, but you are getting a blue screen with a lot of error messages when you are trying to turn on the computer, right?

I am sorry for the same. So you are getting a blue screen with an error message, no?

The ideal paraphrasing statement in response to the user's statement would be:

"I apologize for the inconvenience you've experienced. Thank you for your detailed explanation of the issue. Just to confirm, when you turned on your computer this morning, you saw a blue screen with some error messages, even though you've checked the wires and had a friend reconnect them. Is that correct?"

This response shows empathy, summarizes the user's issue, and seeks to clarify the facts before proceeding with a solution. It is important to make sure that the problem has been correctly understood before trying to solve it. Options 1, 2, and 4 are all grammatically correct, but they are not as professional or complete in terms of summarizing the user's issue and seeking to confirm the facts.

What would be the ideal response if the user says, “I am wondering if you guys will ever resolve my problem.”?


Sure, I'll try my best. Give me the ticket number please.

What is the problem?

Okay, I'll help you. Please tell me what is the problem.

You seem upset. I will look into the matter right away and do my best to help you. Could you please tell me what the concern is?

The ideal response to the user in this situation could be:

"I'm sorry to hear that you're still experiencing issues. I understand your frustration and would like to help you resolve this as quickly as possible. Could you please provide me with your ticket number so that I can have a better understanding of your case?"

This response acknowledges the user's frustration and offers assistance in a polite and professional manner. It also seeks to obtain more information about the user's case to be able to provide more effective support. Option 3 is also acceptable, but it could be seen as slightly less empathetic compared to option 4 which validates the user feelings. Option 1 and 2 could be perceived as dismissive or not taking the user's concern seriously.

How do you pronounce the word 'Technology'?


Tek - NAW - luh - gy

Tek - no - LAW - gy

TAK - naw - lo - gy

TAK - no - lo - gy

The correct pronunciation of the word 'Technology' is: 

Tek - NAH - luh - gee

How do you pronounce the word ‘Technology’? from the following


Tek - NAW - luh - gy

Tek - no - LAW - gy

TAK - naw - lo - gy

TAK - no - lo - gy

The correct pronunciation of the word 'Technology' is: 

Tek - NAH - luh - jee

There is a restaurant ___ my home.


near to

near

opposite to

nearby to

The correct option according to standard usage is: 

There is a restaurant near my home. 

The preposition "near" expresses the proximity of the restaurant to the speaker's home. The preposition "near to" can also be used instead of "near," but "nearby to" and "opposite to" are less common and may be considered non-standard or unnatural in this context.

What is the error message that ___?


you are having?

you receive?

you are seeing?

you getting?

The correct option is:

What is the error message that you are seeing?

The verb "to see" is the most common and appropriate verb to use in this case when asking about an error message, so "seeing" is the correct form. The other options could also be used, but "seeing" is more commonly used in questions about error messages in English.

If you accidentally mispronounce the name of the user and if the user corrects you, how would you continue the conversation?


Oh okay, I'm sorry. (correct name to be pronounced) right?

Okay, It's slightly difficult to pronounce. (pronounce correctly) Is this okay now?

Okay.

I am sorry I did not get your name right. Could you repeat that please?

The ideal way to continue the conversation would be:

"Oh, I'm sorry for mispronouncing your name. Could you please repeat the correct pronunciation for me?"

This response acknowledges the mistake, apologizes for it, and seeks to clarify the correct pronunciation of the user's name. It demonstrates respect for the user and a willingness to make things right. Option 1 is also acceptable, but it would be better to confirm that you have pronounced the name correctly, and option 2 may not sound as formal or professional. Option 3 is not ideal because it doesn't address the mistake and doesn't offer an opportunity to correct the pronunciation.

Identify the incorrect sentence.


I am talking to Jane?

Am I speaking with Jane?

Am I talking to Jane?

Is that Jane?

The correct option is:

I am talking to Jane?

This sentence is grammatically incorrect because it is a statement that is phrased like a question. It requires a verb before the subject to make it a proper question. The other options are all grammatically correct and ask if the speaker is speaking to Jane.
